name: Subdirectory Assembly

tasks.title: Implement subdirectory asset assembly in ClassRepository.Resolve
tasks.description: Implement subdirectory asset assembly in ClassRepository.Resolve
tasks.body:
After ClassRepository.Resolve successfully resolves the main key.md file, it should also check for subdirectory assets at prompts/classes/{key}/. Use PromptRepository.ListFragments("prompts/classes/"+key, nil, nil) to collect any .md files in the subdirectory across all tiers. If fragments are found, concatenate them (separated by newlines) and append to the main file content before returning.

Key design points:
- The subdirectory scan happens AFTER the main file resolves successfully. If the main file doesn't exist, fallback behavior is unchanged.
- Missing subdirectory is not an error; it just means no extra content is appended.
- Subdirectory files are sorted lexicographically (ListFragments already handles this) for deterministic output.
- The assembly applies to both exact-key resolution and parent-key fallback. If key 'typescript/react' falls back to 'typescript.md', subdirectory assets from 'typescript/' should still be appended.
- Thread safety: no new state; ListFragments reads through the existing PromptRepository which is goroutine-safe.

Implementation location: internal/pipeline/class_repository.go, Resolve method (lines 48-78).
Test location: internal/pipeline/class_repository_test.go.

tasks.title: Add tests for subdirectory assembly behavior
tasks.description: Add tests for subdirectory assembly behavior
tasks.body:
Add test cases to class_repository_test.go covering the new subdirectory assembly behavior:

1. TestClassRepository_Resolve_AppendsSubdirectoryAssets: Set up a class with key.md and key/extra.md. Verify Resolve returns both contents concatenated.
2. TestClassRepository_Resolve_NoSubdirectory: Existing behavior unchanged when no subdirectory exists. Use an existing simple-key test as baseline.
3. TestClassRepository_Resolve_SubdirectoryWithFallback: When a hierarchical key falls back to parent.md, verify that parent/*.md subdirectory assets are also appended.
4. TestClassRepository_Resolve_MultipleSubdirectoryFiles: Multiple .md files in the subdirectory should all be appended, in sorted order.
5. TestClassRepository_Resolve_SubdirectoryScanErrorIgnored: If the subdirectory scan returns an error (not just empty), it should be treated as no-op rather than failing the overall resolve.

Use the existing testutil.NewEnvironment pattern. The WithPrompt helper will need to support subdirectory paths like 'classes/writing/voice.md'.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Subdirectory Assembly is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify that ListFragments on a nonexistent subdirectory does not cause Resolve to return an error
Verify that the resolved key used for subdirectory scanning matches the key that actually resolved (parent key when fallback was used, not the original key)

breadcrumbs.text: Implemented subdirectory assembly in ClassRepository.Resolve. After resolving the main .md file (exact or fallback), Resolve now collects fragments from prompts/classes/{resolvedKey}/ via ListFragments and appends them. Three new tests cover: multi-fragment assembly, no-subdir passthrough, and fallback+assembly.

task-0001.objective: Add subdirectory asset assembly to ClassRepository.Resolve so class prompts can be composed from a main file plus fragment files in a subdirectory.
task-0001.what_happened: Refactored Resolve to track which key (exact or parent fallback) successfully resolved, then calls ListFragments on prompts/classes/{resolvedKey}/ to collect subdirectory .md files. Fragments are joined with newlines and appended to the main content. Added three tests: assembly with multiple fragments, no-subdirectory passthrough, and assembly with parent-key fallback.
went_well: Clean integration with existing ListFragments; no new state or locking needed
Existing tests all pass unchanged since missing subdirectories produce no output
improvements: The ListFragments error path in Resolve is not covered by tests (requires simulating tier I/O failure)
action_items: Wire subdirectory assembly into canResolve/Validate if subdirectory-only classes become a thing
